findings: Identity, taxonomy, plumage, adult red iris, strong hooked bill, stocky build, fairly short tail, dark gray legs, and the visible head/beak/wing/leg/tail anatomy are consistent with Red-eyed Vireo and the supplied reference photos.

All printed measurement values match Cornell All About Birds. Audubon and Animal Diversity Web publish larger length and wingspan figures (13–15 cm and 25–28 cm; 15.2 cm and 25.4 cm respectively), so the plate should identify its measurement authority if cross-source consistency is required; this is a source discrepancy, not a material species-identification error.
The behavior, habitat, family, scientific name, and field-mark text agree with the live sources. The plate contains no place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail.

verification_sources.title: Red-eyed Vireo Identification — All About Birds
